_comment: Level-2 reader tasks. Deterministic questions with gold answers grounded byte-for-byte in the L1 tool-only artifacts (results/rtk-codegraph-clap-v1). Answer schema: {facts,files,symbols,call_path,answer}. Scoring is rule-based (bench/reader_tasks.py): count=exact numeric; files/symbols=set-superset exact match; facts=accepted substrings; plus invalid-identifier and alias-leakage checks. No LLM judge.

questions.q: How many errors does this build log report? Put the integer in "answer".
How many warnings does this build log report? Put the integer in "answer".
What is the compiler error code? Put it in "facts".
Which source file has the errors? Put its file name in "files".
Which property triggers the CS8618 non-nullable warning? Put it in "symbols".
How many matches are reported? Put the integer in "answer".
Across how many files? Put the integer in "answer".
Which file has 2 matches? Put its file name in "files".
Name a method shown in SessionService.cs. Put it in "symbols".
Give one exact file path (as shown) that matches. Put it in "files".
Name a value-parser type mentioned. Put it in "symbols".
How many total matching lines are there? Put the integer in "answer".
Give one exact file path (as shown) under src/_derive. Put it in "files".
Which examples directory is referenced by the doc includes? Put the directory name in "facts".
How many symbols did the exploration find? Put the integer in "answer".
Across how many files? Put the integer in "answer".
Name the value-parser type with the most callers. Put it in "symbols".
How many callers does ValueParser have? Put the integer in "answer".
Name the public trait whose parse() method is shown. Put it in "symbols".
In which file is ValueParser defined? Put the file name in "facts".
